Title: Convergent evolution of a metabolic switch between aphid and caterpillar resistance in cereals

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: branch/regulatory step downstream of core benzoxazinoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control/regulatory switch step between DIMBOA-Glc and HDMBOA-Glc pools
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Caterpillar deterrence/antixenosis; defense trade-off with aphid resistance via depletion of DIMBOA-Glc-dependent callose inducibility
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Constitutively expressed in transgenic wheat lines OeBx12_1 and OeBx12_2
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes HDMBOA-Glc accumulation at the expense of DIMBOA-Glc/DIMBOA, reduces callose inducibility and aphid resistance, and increases caterpillar resistance in wheat
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression in wheat; qRT-PCR; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling of leaves and phloem; metabolite infiltration/callose assays; caterpillar choice/no-choice assays; aphid performance assays
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Spodoptera littoralis feeding; Sitobion avenae infestation; chitosan elicitation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Increased caterpillar resistance but decreased aphid resistance and reduced callose inducibility; increased ferulic acid observed
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be used to increase HDMBOA-Glc and reduce caterpillar feeding, but deployment should consider the trade-off of lowered DIMBOA-Glc, weaker callose induction, and greater aphid susceptibility
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used here as a heterologous maize enzyme in wheat to mimic the defense switch; no clear effect on growth, sugars, starch, soluble protein, or major amino acid profiles
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Constitutive expression of ZmBx12 in wheat converted DIMBOA-Glc/DIMBOA to HDMBOA-Glc in leaves and phloem. 2. This metabolic shift suppressed chitosan- and aphid-induced callose deposition. 3. ZmBx12-overexpressing wheat became less attractive to caterpillars but more susceptible to aphids, demonstrating a defense trade-off.

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: branch/regulatory step downstream of core benzoxazinoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates DIMBOA-Glc to HDMBOA-Glc and DIM2BOA-Glc to HDM2BOA-Glc in the presence of S-adenosyl-L-methionine
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control/regulatory switch step likely contributing to herbivore-induced HDMBOA-Glc formation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Caterpillar deterrence/antixenosis associated with switching defense away from DIMBOA-Glc-dependent aphid resistance
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly up-regulated after Spodoptera littoralis herbivory in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Strongly induced in herbivore-damaged leaves; similarly induced in wild-type and OeBx12 lines
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: O-methyltransferase (OMT); related to BX7-like Poaceae OMTs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Encodes a wheat enzyme that produces HDMBOA-Glc from DIMBOA-Glc and likely underlies herbivore-induced DIMBOA-Glc O-methylation in wheat
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Herbivory-induced RNA-seq candidate discovery; qRT-PCR; cloning from wheat cDNA; heterologous expression in Escherichia coli; purified enzyme assay with LC-MS/MS product identification; phylogenetic analysis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely mediates the same trade-off as DIMBOA-Glc O-methylation: improved caterpillar resistance with reduced DIMBOA-Glc pool/callose inducibility and weaker aphid resistance
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A wheat-native target for editing or tuning herbivore-specific defense; reducing TaBx10 may favor aphid resistance, whereas inducible enhancement may favor caterpillar deterrence
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The wheat genome lacks close orthologs of maize ZmBx10/11/12/14; TaBx10 is only distantly related, supporting convergent evolution. In planta functional validation was noted as still needed.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Transcriptomics of caterpillar-damaged wheat identified herbivore-induced OMT candidates, from which TaBx10 was isolated. 2. Recombinant TaBX10 methylated DIMBOA-Glc to HDMBOA-Glc and DIM2BOA-Glc to HDM2BOA-Glc in vitro. 3. Phylogeny showed TaBx10 is not an ortholog of maize Bx10/11/12/14, indicating independent evolution of this benzoxazinoid defense switch.
